doctors.name: Dr. Ahmed Hassan
doctors.bio: Dr. Ahmed Hassan is a cardiologist with over 15 years of experience in treating heart diseases, hypertension, and cardiovascular conditions. He completed his fellowship at Cairo University Hospital.

doctors.name: Dr. Sara Mostafa
doctors.bio: Dr. Sara Mostafa specializes in skin conditions, cosmetic dermatology, and hair treatments. She has 10 years of experience and trained at Ain Shams University.

doctors.name: Dr. Omar Khalil
doctors.bio: Dr. Omar Khalil is an orthopedic surgeon specializing in bone fractures, joint replacements, and sports injuries. He has 12 years of experience.

doctors.name: Dr. Nadia Fouad
doctors.bio: Dr. Nadia Fouad is a pediatrician with 8 years of experience caring for newborns, children, and adolescents. She is known for her gentle and patient approach.

doctors.name: Dr. Youssef Adel
doctors.bio: Dr. Youssef Adel is a general practitioner handling routine checkups, chronic disease management, and referrals. Available most days with flexible slots.

faqs.question: Where is the clinic located?
faqs.answer: Cairo Medical Center is located at 15 Tahrir Square, Downtown Cairo, Egypt.

faqs.question: What are the clinic working hours?
faqs.answer: The clinic is open Sunday to Thursday from 9am to 5pm. We are closed on Fridays and Saturdays.

faqs.question: How do I book an appointment?
faqs.answer: You can book an appointment by telling us your preferred doctor, date, and time. We will check availability and confirm your booking.

faqs.question: Can I cancel or reschedule my appointment?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can cancel or reschedule your appointment at any time. Please provide your name and appointment details and we will handle it immediately.

faqs.question: Is there parking available?
faqs.answer: Yes, free parking is available for patients in the building's underground garage.

faqs.question: Do you accept insurance?
faqs.answer: We accept most major Egyptian insurance providers including GlobeMed, AXA, and Allianz. Please bring your insurance card on the day of your visit.

faqs.question: What should I bring to my appointment?
faqs.answer: Please bring your national ID, insurance card if applicable, and any previous medical reports or test results relevant to your condition.

faqs.question: Is there an emergency service?
faqs.answer: Yes, our emergency line is available 24/7. You can reach us at +20 2 9999 0000 for urgent cases.
